summary:
During mandibular distolingual extension for dental impressions, the inferior belly of the lateral pterygoid muscle restricts overextension due to its role in jaw functions like depression and protrusion. This limitation ensures accurate impression formation without compromising functional integrity. Answer: Inferior belly of the lateral pterygoid muscle (\(\boxed{\text{C}}\)). Summary sentence count: 2

The summary accurately captures two critical aspects from Agent1’s response—how this anatomical structure influences precise extensions needed clinically while ensuring proper functioning—and correctly identifies "Inferior belly of the lateral pterygoid muscle" with answer choice \( \boxed{\text{C}} \). It maintains brevity within specified constraints but still conveys essential information pertinent to understanding why such anatomic limitations are important contextually.
